summary: Tail-recursive zero-counter scan over a u64 slice (count_zeros_at) whose postcondition is "∃ three strictly-ascending indices holding 0 ↔ count ≥ 3"; on top of the standard counter-correct-vs-Nat-oracle proof (strong induction on size − i.toNat, with count_zeros_from_le bounding the counter by remaining indices), the iff is closed by two symmetric three-step ladders — a forward ladder one_zero_from → two_zeros_from → three_zeros_from (counter ≥ k ⇒ ∃ k ascending witnesses, each level cases on `numbers[start] = 0` and consumes the previous ladder for the suffix) and a reverse ladder count_ge_one_of_zero → count_ge_two_of_two_zeros → count_ge_three_of_three_zeros (∃ k ascending witnesses ⇒ counter ≥ k, inducting on the gap `i − start` and case-splitting `start = i`), then projected at start=0 into the two top-level soundness/completeness theorems via a final u64 `>= 3` comparison.
